critic_raw:
The 1D CNN treats irregular visit sequences as uniformly spaced positions, distorting temporal relationships. The time interval feature is passed to the LSTM but the CNN operates on raw sequence positions without temporal calibration — a patient with weekly visits followed by a 3-month gap will have that gap misrepresented as equal to weekly intervals.

The CNN-LSTM input format is ambiguous — whether it receives raw event sequences or pre-aggregated features is unspecified, making it unclear whether the model genuinely processes temporal dynamics.

The 0.5-year validation window versus 1.5-year test window creates asymmetric evaluation where hyperparameter selection is tuned on a shorter, potentially unrepresentative period.
all_issues_raised: The 1D CNN treats irregular visit sequences as uniformly spaced, distorting temporal relationships; the irregular time interval feature fed to the LSTM does not correct for this within the CNN's local pattern extraction.

The CNN-LSTM input format is ambiguous — whether it receives raw event sequences or pre-aggregated features determines whether the model is genuinely learning sequential dynamics or just applying a complex architecture to the same features as the baseline.

The 0.5-year validation window versus 1.5-year test window creates an asymmetric evaluation where hyperparameter selection is tuned on a shorter, potentially unrepresentative period relative to the final test distribution.

round_transcripts.critic:
The 1D CNN treats irregular visit sequences as uniformly spaced positions, distorting temporal relationships. The time interval feature is passed to the LSTM but the CNN operates on raw sequence positions without temporal calibration — a patient with weekly visits followed by a 3-month gap will have that gap misrepresented as equal to weekly intervals.

The CNN-LSTM input format is ambiguous — whether it receives raw event sequences or pre-aggregated features is unspecified, making it unclear whether the model genuinely processes temporal dynamics.

The 0.5-year validation window versus 1.5-year test window creates asymmetric evaluation where hyperparameter selection is tuned on a shorter, potentially unrepresentative period.
round_transcripts.defender:
The temporal irregularity concern is partially addressed by including time-since-last-admission as an explicit feature in the input vector at each sequence position, allowing the LSTM to learn time-weighted attention. The CNN's local pattern extraction operates on visit proximity in sequence space, which is meaningful even without exact temporal calibration — patterns like multiple hospitalizations within a short sequence window carry clinical signal.

The CNN-LSTM receives per-visit feature vectors as a sequence, not pre-aggregated features — this is the definition of a sequential model. The logistic regression receives the same underlying information in aggregated form. The comparison is precisely between raw sequential input and aggregated features, which is the scientific question.

The validation/test asymmetry is a consequence of the temporal split design and is common in clinical predictive modeling. The validation set is used for early stopping and hyperparameter selection, not for reporting final performance. The test set is the authoritative evaluation regardless of its length relative to validation.

round_transcripts.critic:
The defense clarifies that the LSTM receives time-since-last-admission as a feature, which partially addresses temporal calibration at the LSTM level. However, the CNN still operates on position indices without temporal calibration — the CNN's learned local filters will capture proximity in visit count, not proximity in time. For patients with highly irregular visit intervals, CNN patterns may not generalize. This concern is partially resolved but not fully.

The input format clarification resolves the second issue — the CNN-LSTM receives per-visit sequences, not pre-aggregated features.
round_transcripts.defender: The CNN's position-based filters capture clinical event co-occurrence patterns within short visit windows, which is a valid and commonly used representation even under irregular intervals. Time-since-last-visit in the LSTM compensates for irregularity at the sequence level. The design is internally consistent.

all_issues_adjudicated: The 1D CNN treats irregular visit sequences as uniformly spaced, distorting temporal relationships; the irregular time interval feature fed to the LSTM does not correct for this within the CNN's local pattern extraction.
